poems.title: Time Travel
poems.author: Chidhambaram (24UCO241)
content: Through ages past, I wander free
A traveller of time, wild and carefree
I dance with dinosaurs, in a by gone era’s sway
And stroll with wisdom, in a future day
The clock ticks on, as I roam and play
Visiting eras, in a timeless way
I meet the past, and greet the future’s gaze
A journey through time, in endless daze
With every step, a new tale unfolds
Of love, loss, and dreams, forever told
I weave through centuries, like a thread in time
A time traveller, forever aligned
In this realm of chronology, I am free
To explore, discover, and be me
No bounds confine, no limits define
A traveller of time, forever divine.

poems.title: This City
poems.author: Hepcy Pricilla J J
content: Paradoxical is the word I would use to
describe my relationship with this city,
It makes me both frustrated and contented
with its unpredictable weather.
It drains me, yet it opens its heart to share my sorrows.
It gives me a headache, yet it also brings me solace.
It laughs at me and also laughs with me.
It prompts me to question my existence
and gives me a reason to live.
It annoys me to drive on unpredictable roads with heavy traffic.
Crowded public transportation often making me late to everything,
but it also provides comfort amidst the crowds.
It pushes me down to my lowest,
but also teaches me lessons that could never be learned in any classroom.
It makes me meet people who rip me apart,
and make me lose all hope in humanity
but also gives me people that make me feel
like the best version of myself giving hope.
It covers me with its darkness
but also helps me see the light at the end of the tunnel
It leaves me with unanswered questions
but also answers questions I have never thought about
It makes me question my worth and talent,
but it also offers me opportunities and allows me to shine.
It makes me feel confined and suffocated here, unable to escape.
Yet, after a long day,
it's the place I yearn to return to and relax in my bed.

poems.title: Her
poems.author: Kainat Jameel, The Women University Multan
content: She wandered through the nights alone,
With heavy steps, her heart like stone.
The world around, so cold and steep,
Yet in her soul, a dream to keep.
She climbed the hills of endless pain,
And chased the light through pouring rain.
But every path would turn and twist,
Leaving her lost in shadows' mist.
She longs for peace to ease her mind,
A place of rest she cannot find.
Will peace arrive, or fade away?
She wonders with each passing day.
The stars above, they whisper low,
But do they tell of peace to grow?
Or is it just a distant dream,
Forever out of reach, it seems?
And so she cries through sleepless nights,
To lift the weight, to feel more light.
Each tear she sheds, a silent plea,
For peace to set her spirit free.
Her spirit trapped in shadows deep,
Where whispered truths and secrets sleep.
Yet still she dreams of finding peace,
A quiet place where chains release.
Will she break free, or stay confined?
The answer lingers, undefined.
But in her eyes, a spark remains,
A quiet fire against the chains.
And as she breathes beneath the night,
With hope that flickers, dim but bright,
She feels the weight of endless fight,
And wonders if she'll find the light.
Will tomorrow bring her heart’s desire,
Or leave her lost, alone, and tired?
In silence, deep, her spirit strains,
Will peace be hers, or just remain?

poems.title: The Buried Voices of Palestine
poems.author: S Syed Anas
content: Neither the sun nor the alarm wakes us up.
However, the sun does rise -
But we are already awake before dawn,
Startled by the terrifying sounds of bombs
As everyone greets dawn with warm
smiles from their family,
We face the pain -
And hear the cries of our neighbors,
Our community soaked in sorrow.
There isn't sufficient water in our vessels
To satisfy even our hunger.
Yet, there is abundant blood
Flowing across our open fields.
Sometimes, our children fill their stomachs
With nothing but green grass,
While others elsewhere
Carelessly throw food in the thrash.
If a person who cannot see is called blind,
And one who cannot hear is deaf -
Then truly, there are billions among the living
Who see nothing and hear nothing.
Some of us have lost our hands,
our legs ,our feet-
But one thing is certain:
We have not lost our hearts.
While Muslims across the world pray
five times a day,
Here in Palestine -
We pray six.

poems.title: Dreams
poems.author: Abdul Razzak M, Madras Christian College
content: "Hi" said an invisible man
One day on elegant night
When I was fast asleep on my bed
Tired of tedious day
Where are you?, I said
I don't know where the sound comes from,
I don't how it is, but
What do you think,
I do know who it is,
I do know what it is.
How can I help you? He said.
I do know what I'm seein
What do you think,
There was a second mother and a
Youth wearin a white coat.
He was sitting in front of me
And she was vertical beside him,
What do you think he is , if you ask me,
I rather suggest him as A doc.
There was patients of roasters standby
Like cages of clouds,
There was class of cleavers ,
Like in butchers shop,
All arranged by the side of my left limb,
What do you think,
Felt like, i came to dining of hell.
Slowly and suddenly,
A peculiar humming sound was heard,
The humming started roaming my hear,
Humming,humming,humming.....'n bang,
What do you think,
It was from king of hell.
Sir....!? Asked he,
"Are you here, can you hear".!? said the king,
Want a loan..!?
What do you think,
The question felt like a Brian without skull.
I was confused,
Felt like blinded,
Trapped in maze and captured,
What do you think,
The name of the place is ICCI reservoir.
You may ask ,
What it means to dream ,
Who it means,
How it means,
Where it means ,What do you think,
I may ask,
Is this a dream!!!!.

poems.title: Women
poems.author: L.K. Jothirekha, MCC Tambaram
content: Women are treated as slaves
A rule was given by the male once;
They are strong and brave;
which never destroyed their fame;
Men considered women as weak,
but the women shined in unique;
they strive hard,
with the men considered as mad;
They are the burning fire,
which given the men the title as spier.
